data.title: NYU’s Aswath Damodaran: Markets will rally until risks show up in earnings and economy
data.description:
Concerns about stock market valuations reaching unsustainable levels have intensified as major indices hit new highs. Aswath Damodaran, professor of finance at the NYU School of Business, suggested that markets may be more trustworthy than experts when it comes to predicting future performance.

“For the last 10 years, we’ve had this conversation over and over again,” Damodaran said in an interview with CNBC. “Markets (SP500), (DJI), (COMP:IND) reach a new high. We say, ‘are markets crazy?’ And experts like me come on, and we say, ‘markets are too rich.’”

Damodaran’s perspective challenges the conventional wisdom that has dominated market analysis in recent years. He noted that over the past three to four years, he has come to believe that “markets maybe are more trustworthy on this than experts are, that their markets seem to figure things out.” This represents a significant shift from his previous stance on market valuations.

According to Damodaran, the market has experienced two distinct phases this year. “Through April 8, tech (XLK) especially was beaten up. The Mag 7 (MAGS) collectively lost, I think, $2T in market cap just in that first three months,” he said, highlighting the substantial volatility that has characterized recent market movements.

When asked about the Federal Reserve’s impact on markets, Damodaran expressed skepticism about the significance of rate cuts. “They don’t even affect the one thing they’re supposed to affect, which is interest rates,” he said. “They might affect the Fed funds rate, but treasury rates (US10Y), (US2Y), (US30Y) themselves have a life of their own.”

He predicted that even with Fed rate cuts, treasury bond rates would likely remain above 4% as long as inflation stays around 2.5-2.7%.

Damodaran also attributed recent market movements to a continuously shifting expectations game that “keeps getting reset for this market, and sometimes just delivering what is expected becomes an unexpected plus,” he said.

This pattern explains why markets often react positively to news that merely meets rather than exceeds expectations, creating what Damodaran describes as “a very strange twist on the expectations game.”

data.title: Elon Musk set to take on Microsoft with new company
data.description:
• Elon Musk asked people on Friday to join his artificial intelligence company xAI in an effort to take on software giant Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT).
  • “Join @xAI and help build a purely AI software company called Macrohard,” Musk wrote in a post on X. “It’s a tongue-in-cheek name, but the project is very real! In principle, given that software companies like Microsoft do not themselves manufacture any physical hardware, it should be possible to simulate them entirely with AI.”
  • A limited liability company known as Macrohard was filed with the U.S. Patent Office recently. The filing was attributed to xAI and was filed with the U.S. agency on August 1, according to PCMag.
  • Microsoft did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

data.title: Financial stocks gap up after Fed chief Powell's dovish remarks
data.description:
Shares of fintechs and banks rose with the broader stock market Friday after investors cheered Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell's speech opening the door to an interest-rate cut next month.

The Financial Select Sector SPDR ETF (NYSEARCA:XLF) gained 1.6% in midafternoon trading, roughly in-line with the S&P 500 Index's rise. The leveraged Direxion Daily Financial Bull 3X Shares ETF (FAS) climbed 5%.

The intraday gains come after Powell's dovish remarks. A shifting balance of risks "may warrant adjusting our policy stance," he said in his widely anticipated address at the Kansas City's Fed Central Bank Symposium at Jackson Hole, Wyoming.

Among the Wall Street banks, Goldman Sachs (GS) was the biggest gainer, jumping 3.6% at 3:25 p.m. ET, followed by Citigroup's (C) 3% increase. From there, Bank of America (BAC) +2.8%, Morgan Stanley (MS) +2.8%, Wells Fargo (WFC) +1.8% and JPMorgan Chase (JPM) +1.6%.

On the regional lenders, U.S. Bancorp (USB) +4.6%, PNC Financial Services Group (PNC) +4.1%, Flagstar Financial (FLG) +6.2%, KeyCorp (KEY) +5%, Comerica (CMA) +4.8%, Western Alliance Bancorporation (WAL) +7.2%, Huntington Bancshares (HBAN) +4.4% and Webster Financial (WBS) +5.6%.

Fintechs were especially strong, with Pagaya Technologies (PGY) +13%, Block (XYZ) +6.7%, LendingClub (LC) +6.6%, Rocket Companies (RKT) +9.9%, Upstart Holdings (UPST) +7.5%, Blend Labs (BLND) +8.2%, LendingTree (TREE) +9.5% and SoFi Technologies (SOFI) +7.3%.

Elsewhere in the financials space, stocks of real estate brokerages, homebuilders, and mortgage lenders also soared.

data.title: Paramount Skydance reportedly targeting its massive one-time job cuts in early November
data.description:
Paramount Skydance (NASDAQ:PSKY) is expected to conduct a massive round of layoffs in early November, according to a report by The New York Post on Friday, citing sources.

One source familiar with the matter told The Post that company president Jeff Shell, formerly the top boss at NBCUniversal, has told managers at Paramount Pictures, CBS, MTV, and Showtime to start compiling "kill lists."

The lists will be submitted in October, and layoffs will start in early November, sources told NYP.

The report said the cuts will coincide with the company's third-quarter earnings, as well as an investor presentation by new management.

"It’s going to be a bloodbath. It’s going to be ugly," according to a source who told NYP.

In a press conference last week, Shell told journalists that the cuts will be "painful" but will occur in one shot, unlike multiple rounds of layoffs under the previous management.

“We do not want to be a company that has layoffs every quarter,” Shell said at the LA presser last week. "So, it’s going to be painful… It is important for us to get done what we’re doing in one big thing and then be done with it."

data.title: Intel in focus as Trump administration may unveil equity stake details soon: report
data.description:
Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) was in focus on Friday as Bloomberg reported the Trump Administration could unveil the details on its equity stake in the beleaguered chipmaker soon.

Details of the announcement could happen as soon as Friday, the news outlet added, citing people familiar with the plan.

Intel shares rose nearly 5% in midday trading.

President Trump said on Friday that Intel had “agreed to do it and I think it’s a great deal for them.” He added that “Intel has been left behind” in terms of its competitors.

Despite the president's comments, details on the deal are still being worked out.

Earlier this month, reports emerged that the U.S. government was discussing taking a 10% stake in the company. Taking a 10% stake in Intel would require about $10.5B and make the U.S. government the largest shareholder. The Administration is considering using funds from the U.S. CHIPS and Science Act to pay for the investment.

Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick confirmed this week the Administration was in discussions over using CHIPS funding to invest in Intel. If the U.S. purchased the 10% stake, it would become the company's largest shareholder.

Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent also said the plan to acquire a stake in Intel to help it develop a functioning foundry domestically is crucial for national security.

The Lip Bu Tan-led Intel recently scored a win when Japan's SoftBank (OTCPK:SFTBY) said it would invest $2B in the company at $23 per share. The company is also reportedly talking to other large investors about taking a stake.

(This story has been updated to include comments from President Trump.)

data.title: AT&T poised to end in red after six-session rally
data.description:
AT&T (NYSE:T) snapped six consecutive sessions of gains on Friday as the stock plunged 2.14% to trade at $28.81 during afternoon trading.

Since August 14, the stock has closed in the green every day until August 21, during which the telecom provider gained over 2.40%. On a year-to-date basis, it has increased by over 30% compared to an 8.3% rise in the broader markets.

As per Seeking Alpha’s quant rating and analysts, it is recommended to Hold T. It has a score of 3.46 out of 5. The company has been graded A+ for profitability but holds an F rating for growth.

However, Seeking Alpha and Wall Street analysts share a consensus rating of Buy for AT&T.

Seeking Alpha analyst Daniel Jones maintained his Strong Buy rating on the stock, arguing that AT&T is a cash-generating, undervalued business with strong fundamentals and consistent growth.

“Valuation models suggest 12%–19% annualized upside through 2027, making AT&T a compelling, low-risk opportunity for long-term investors,” it said.

Another analyst, Long Player, also echoed a similar view on the company, saying that the company is well-placed as a reliable growth and income opportunity, with further stock price recovery expected.

They argued that cell phones and the internet have become modern-day “utilities” that are essential for nearly everyone and will be somewhat recession-resistant. Given its scale and global diversification, the analyst said, AT&T stands out as a compelling growth and income opportunity.

“AT&T remains a "Strong Buy" as a growth and income idea. The recovery potential that began this idea is largely over with. But the promise of growing the business in low to maybe medium single-digit percentages (including an occasional well-thought-out acquisition) makes this a decent lower-risk growth and income idea,” the analyst concluded.

data.title: Dow surges to record high as Wall Street rallies on Powell’s Jackson Hole remarks
data.description:
Wall Street jumped sharply on Friday, with the Dow hitting a fresh all-time high as investors welcomed Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell’s remarks at Jackson Hole.

The tech focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) has popped up by 1.9%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) has moved up by 1.5%, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) has pushed up by 1.9%.

Across sectors, 10 of the 11 S&P groups are trading higher, with Consumer Discretionary and Energy leading the advance. Consumer Staples, meanwhile, is the session’s weakest performer. 

Over in the bond market yields are shifting lower as investors buy bonds. The longer-end U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) has declined by 6 basis points to 4.26%, and the shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) has fell 10 basis points to 3.68%.

At Jackson Hole, Powell remarked that a shifting balance of risks "may warrant adjusting our policy stance," signaling a potential willingness to lower the central bank’s policy rate if conditions justify it.

"Risks to inflation are tilted to the upside, and risks to employment to the downside — a challenging situation," Powell added.

As a result, the odds of a rate cut in September rose as the chance of a September cut was at ~71% before the speech and rose to ~93% after it.

"Powell's dovish Jackson Hole comments suggest the Federal Reserve is ready to cut interest rates in September, which is just what investors were hoping to hear, given the recent slowdown in the labor market. While there is still one more employment report before the September meeting, it's clear the Fed has enough data under its belt to justify a September cut," David Laut, chief investment officer at Abound Financial said.

"Jerome Powell's speech in Jackson Hole paves the way for a September rate cut. Whether it will be 25 basis points or 50 basis points is still unknown, but the idea that the Fed is open to cutting rates is bullish for stocks," Paul Stanley, chief investment officer, Granite Bay Wealth Management stated.

Aside from the headline of Powell's speech at Jackson Hole, the economic calendar is light otherwise.

As for stocks that are on the move, shares of Intuit (INTU) slipped 4.9% after the financial software firm reported its latest quarterly earnings.

data.title: Catalyst Watch: Spotlight on Nvidia, Alibaba, PCE inflation read, and the return of Bed Bath & Beyond
data.description:
Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

Readers are invited to take part in Seeking Alpha's Weekly News Quiz 

 

Monday - August 25

  • Volatility watch - Opendoor (OPEN) and Asset Entities (ASST) are set up for a volatile week of trading based on options volume. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include Avidia (AVBC), QMMM Holdings (QMMM), and Indivior (INDV). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day Relative Strength Index include Bragg Gaming Group Inc (BRAG), Monday.com (MNDY), and Vtex (VTEX).
  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include PDD Holdings (PDD).
  • IPO watch - No new IPOs are expected to be launched next week in the runup to the typical Labor Day slowdown. IPO quiet period expires on Figma (FIG), Ambiq Micro (AMBQ), and Avidia Bancorp (AVBC) to free up analysts to post ratings.
  • Dividend watch - Companies that have an ex-dividend date coming next week include Johnson & Johnson (JNJ), S&P Global (SPGI), Hyatt (H), and Tyson Foods (TSN).
  • All week - The VMware Explore 2025 event will take place in Las Vegas. The VMware’s flagship technology conference under Broadcom (AVGO) ownership is one of the most influential enterprise IT gatherings of the year. A major highlight will be the keynote by Broadcom President and CEO Hock Tan on August 26, focusing on "Shaping the Future of Private Cloud and AI Innovation."
  • 7:15 p.m. New York Federal Reserve Bank President John Williams will give a keynote talk before the Bank of Mexico Centennial Conference.

 

Tuesday - August 26

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Bank of Montreal (BMO), Bank of Nova Scotia (BNS), MongoDB (MDB), and Okta (OKTA).
  • All day - Starbucks (SBUX) officially brings back the wildly successful Pumpkin Spice Latte product along with the rest of the coffee chain's fall menu, which will also include seasonal favorites like the Pumpkin Cream Cold Brew, Iced Pumpkin Cream Chai, and Pecan Crunch Oatmilk Latte.
  • All day - Companies due to participate at the Jefferies Semiconductor, IT Hardware & Communication Technology Summit include Tower Semiconductor (TSEM), Amdocs (DOX), NXP Semiconductors (NXPI), and ASML Holding (ASML).
  • 8:30 a.m. The July Durable Goods Orders will be released. Economists forecast a 1.2% year-over-year decline after the 9.3% decline in June.
  • 9:00 a.m. Economists have the release of the S&P/Case-Shiller House Price Index circled as a key update for the housing market.

 

Wednesday - August 27

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Nvidia (NVDA), Royal Bank of Canada (RY), Crowdstrike (CRWD), Snowflake (SNOW), Veeva Systems (VEEV), Agilent (A), Pure Storage (PSTG), Five Below (FIVE), Williams-Sonoma (WSM), and HP (HPQ). Options trading implies a double-digit move for Kohl's (KSS) and Abercrombie & Fitch (ANF) after their earnings are released.
  • All day - Companies due to participate at the two-day Deutsche Bank Technology Conference include Fortinet (FTNT), Cisco (CSCO), CoreWeave (CRWV), Arista Networks (ANET), Microsoft (MSFT), and ServiceNow (NOW).
  • All day - Rocket Lab Corp (RKLB), SoFi Technologies (SOFI), and Affirm Holdings (AFRM) will be added to the MSCI ACWI Index.
  • All day - The U.S. will begin to apply a 50% duty rate on certain Indian imports unless an extension or deal is worked out.
  • All day - Circana will release its July report on video game hardware and software sales.
  • All day - It is the five-year anniversary of XPeng's (XPEV) IPO.
  • 11:45 a.m. - Richmond Federal Reserve Bank President Thomas Barkin will speak before the Greensboro Chamber of Commerce State of Our Community Luncheon 2025.
  • 2:00 p.m. - Nvidia (NVDA) will hold its earnings conference call. AMD (AMD) and Taiwan Semi (TSM) have the closest trading correlation to Nvidia. Suppliers that derive a significant portion of revenue from the chipmaker and typically trade directionally with it after earnings include Fabrinet (FN) and Micron (MU).

 

Thursday - August 28

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Dell Technologies (DELL), Marvell Technology (MRVL), Autodesk (ADSK), Dollar General (DG), Li Auto (LI), Best Buy (BBY) and Dick's Sporting Goods (DKS). Options trading implies a double-digit move for Victoria's Secret (VSCO) and Affirm (AFRM) after their earnings are released.
  • 11:45 a.m. - Intel (INTC) CFO David Zinsner will participate in a fireside chat at the Deutsche Bank Technology Conference. It will be one of the first public appearances by Intel management since the company secured a $2B investment from Japan's SoftBank (OTCPK:SFTBY) (OTCPK:SFTBF) and learned that the U.S. government may acquire a stake.

 

Friday - August 29

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Alibaba (BABA).
  • 8:30 a.m. The core PCE price index report for July will be released. The Fed's favored inflation gauge was up 2.8% year-over-year in June and 2.7% in May.
  • All day - The U.S. "de minimis" exemption, which has allowed most imports valued at $800 or less to enter duty-free, is set to end for nearly all countries.
  • All day - It is the last trading session before the first major weekend of the college football season that features several marquee match-ups, including Ohio State vs. Texas and Florida State vs. Alabama. The sports betting sector is expected to see an early surge of volume this year, which may increase investor interest in DraftKings (DKNG) and FanDuel owner Flutter Entertainment (FLUT).
  • All day - Beyond Inc. (BYON) will change its corporate name to Bed Bath & Beyond (BBBY). The company currently owns Bed Bath & Beyond, Overstock, buybuy BABY, and other related brands and websites, as well as a blockchain asset portfolio.

Seeking Alpha Editor Josh Fineman contributed to this story

data.title: Consumer Discretionary jumps 3% as Powell sparks rally: Here are 10 stocks to watch
data.description:
After lagging the broader market for much of 2025, the Consumer Discretionary sector came roaring back on Friday, climbing more than 3% to lead all S&P 500 (SP500) segments.

The rally followed upbeat investor reaction to Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell’s latest comments at the Jackson Hole Economic Symposium. 

Against this backdrop, Seeking Alpha has highlighted the top 10 U.S. Consumer Discretionary stocks that stand out across three ratings systems — SA Quant Ratings, SA Analyst Ratings, and Wall Street Analyst Ratings — with each name carrying a Buy or Strong Buy designation. 

All companies on the list have market capitalizations above $1B, and the ranking is based on year-to-date performance: 

No. 1:National Vision Holdings (EYE), +130.7% YTD.

No. 2:GigaCloud Technology (GCT), +61.7% YTD.

No. 3: Stride (LRN), +61.4% YTD.

No. 4:Adtalem Global Education (ATGE), +46.2% YTD.

No. 5:Laureate Education (LAUR), +44.1% YTD.

No. 6: Hasbro (HAS), +42% YTD.

No. 7: Urban Outfitters (URBN), +34.6% YTD.

No. 8: Coursera (COUR), +32.7% YTD.

No. 9: BorgWarner Inc. (BWA), +31.6% YTD.

No. 10: Grand Canyon Education (LOPE), +25.3% YTD.

For reference, Seeking Alpha's Quant system is driven by powerful computer processing and SA's special 'Quantamental' analysis. It awards grades to stocks based on collective value, growth, profitability, earnings per share revisions, and price momentum metrics. 

For further diversification towards the retail space, see below a grouping of exchange traded funds. 

Consumer Discretionary ETFs: (NYSEARCA:XLY), (NYSEARCA:VCR), (NYSEARCA:FXD), (NYSEARCA:FDIS), (NYSEARCA:RSPD), and (NYSEARCA:RXI).

data.title: U.S. judge pauses changes to Affordable Care Act marketplace
data.description:
The U.S. District Court of Maryland on Friday issued a preliminary injunction halting the Trump administration from making changes to the Affordable Care Act's health insurance marketplace days before the changes were set to take effect.

U.S. District Judge Brendan Hurson in Baltimore sided with plaintiffs’ arguments that more than 2.2M could lose healthcare coverage due to the changes planned under the “Marketplace Integrity and Affordability” rule, which was scheduled for implementation on Aug. 25.

In the lawsuit filed by the legal group Democracy Forward on behalf of parties including the city of Chicago and Doctors for America in July, the judge paused changes as the case moves forward.

Hurson halted the implementation of almost all portions of the rule that plaintiffs challenged in court, including those related to higher fees and additional scrutiny targeted at low-income enrollees.

“By blocking the Trump-Vance administration’s dangerous rule, the court has protected millions of Americans from losing coverage, facing higher costs, and being denied essential benefits,” said Dr. Christine Petrin, Board President, Doctors for America.

Operators in the ACA marketplace include Centene (NYSE:CNC), Molina Healthcare (MOH), UnitedHealth (NYSE:UNH), Oscar Health (NYSE:OSCR), Elevance Health (ELV), CVS Health (NYSE:CVS), and Cigna (CI).
